intent: Four fixes: remove roadmap/map view, tighten card widths, fix bucket-not-found error, fix dialog scrollbars. Round 2 re-review focused on verifying two Round 1 fixes (storage RLS ownership guard + list-only view test assertions).
previous_fixes: ceacf73f - fix(storage): add user-ownership guard to learning-path-covers RLS policies
67f347e4 - test(learning-path): add list-only view assertions after roadmap removal

findings.title: Header comment describes userId-folder convention but learning-path-covers uses flat keys
findings.why_it_matters: The file header (lines 12-14) says 'Path convention: {userId}/{recordId}/{filename}' and describes foldername-based RLS. The new learning-path-covers bucket intentionally diverges from this pattern (flat keys, filename-based ownership). The header should note the exception to prevent confusion during future maintenance.
evidence: storage-setup.sql lines 258-283: learning-path-covers uses split_part(storage.filename(name), '.', 1) instead of foldername
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the file header acknowledging the learning-path-covers divergence from the folder convention, or add a separate section comment above the learning-path-covers block explaining the flat-key pattern.

residual_risks: The scale-105 hover effect on gradient preset buttons may be partially clipped by the new overflow-hidden on the grid container when a button is selected. Users see a subtle visual cutoff on edges. Low impact — the primary fix (no scrollbars) is achieved.
testing_gaps: No test verifies the gap-entry rendering path in the list-only view. Gap entries have courseId === '' and require special handling. Existing tests may cover this in other test files.
deployment_notes: The storage-setup.sql must be run against each Supabase environment (local dev + production) to create the learning-path-covers bucket and RLS policies. Without this migration, cover uploads will continue to fail with 'bucket not found'.

fixes_verified.detail: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE policies now enforce auth.uid() match against learning_paths.user_id via subquery lookup. Key format ${pathId}.jpg (confirmed in pathCoverUpload.ts:105) is compatible with split_part() extraction. NULL subquery result correctly blocks unauthorized operations.

New test properly asserts 'Courses' heading, '0 of 1 completed' progress summary, and negative assertions for removed 'Your Roadmap' heading and 'Map' toggle. Mock data (1 entry, 0 completed) supports the expected assertions.
